old_claim.note: Herbalism provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct herbalism dependency remains because the node is scoped to Alexandrian anatomical observation and dissection rather than medicinal plant practice.

source_shape.source_locator: Gross anatomy section: Britannica anchors the first substantial factual basis of anatomy to Alexandrian dissection by Herophilus around 300 BCE.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source supports anatomical dissection and body-structure study, not herbal materia medica as a prerequisite.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Herbalism belongs to medicine and pharmacology neighbors; retaining it here would conflate therapeutic plant knowledge with anatomical investigation.

ontology_before: Anatomy (Early Studies) depended on Herbalism as an unsourced enabling edge.
ontology_after: Anatomy (Early Studies) keeps writing, philosophy, and bronze-working context while herbalism remains outside the direct prerequisite set.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: herbalism is no longer a direct prerequisite for anatomy_early_studies.
would_reject_if: The node were rescoped to early medical therapeutics rather than anatomical study.
A stronger source showed herbal materia medica directly enabled Alexandrian dissection.
A separate herbal_anatomy_theory node were added and intentionally bridged these scopes.
short_reason: Herbalism is therapeutic context, not anatomical method.
